name: Ars quadripartita — Phytoglyphica (147 plants), Fungiglyphica (86 fungi), Animaglyphica (80 animals), and Therapeutica (10 therapies) as distinct Frobenius categories with inter-domain functorial mappings

phase_0.domain_name: Ars quadripartita — Phytoglyphica (147 plants), Fungiglyphica (86 fungi), Animaglyphica (80 animals), and Therapeutica (10 therapies) as distinct Frobenius categories with inter-domain functorial mappings
phase_0.boundary_condition: Therapeutica — the 10 therapies that close the system by mapping any biological state to a therapeutic outcome, containing all four domains within a single curative frame.

VINIT.chosen_element: Ars quadripartita — the uninscribed fourfold domain before any plant, fungus, animal, or therapy is named
VINIT.justification: The void from which all four categories emerge; the seed before any glyph is written.

TANCH.chosen_element: Therapeutica — the 10 therapies that bound and close the system
TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor: every plant, fungus, animal, and therapy is contained within the therapeutic frame. No operation escapes the curative boundary.

AFWD.chosen_element: Phytoglyphica → Animaglyphica — a forward morphism from plant to animal via ingestion or ecological interaction
AFWD.justification: Directed transformation: a plant is consumed by an animal, transferring structural properties (e.g., alkaloid → neurotransmitter). The ergative case of the biological grammar.

AREV.chosen_element: Animaglyphica → Fungiglyphica — a reverse morphism from animal to fungus via decomposition
AREV.justification: Descent transformation: an animal dies and is decomposed by fungi, returning matter to the fungal network. The contravariant descent of the biological cycle.

CLINK.chosen_element: Phytoglyphica → Fungiglyphica → Animaglyphica — sequential chaining across all three kingdoms
CLINK.justification: Composition: a plant is decomposed by a fungus, which is then consumed by an animal. The agglutinative chain of trophic levels.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: A single species within any kingdom — e.g., Atropa belladonna in Phytoglyphica
IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the species that knows itself as a distinct glyph. The identity element of the biological type lattice.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: A therapeutic protocol that branches into two distinct arms: a plant-based arm (Phytoglyphica) and a fungal-based arm (Fungiglyphica)
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: one therapy splits into two distinct material paths — e.g., a single disease treated by either a plant alkaloid or a fungal metabolite.

FFUSE.chosen_element: The combined therapeutic outcome that reconstitutes the original disease state as cured
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two arms (plant and fungal) converge on the same curative endpoint. FFUSE(FSPLIT(disease)) = cured disease.

EVALT.chosen_element: A successful therapeutic outcome — disease resolved, patient restored
EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: the therapy works. The true branch of the therapeutic fork.

EVALF.chosen_element: A failed therapeutic outcome — disease persists or worsens
EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: the therapy fails. The false branch of the therapeutic fork.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: A paradoxical therapeutic state where the disease is both present and absent — e.g., a chronic condition managed but not cured, or a placebo response
ENGAGR.justification: Both simultaneously: the disease is held in a state of suspended resolution. The paradice of the chronic patient.

IFIX.chosen_element: A permanent record in the Therapeutica — a therapy that has been canonized as a fixed protocol
IFIX.justification: Irreversible fixation: once a therapy is inscribed in the Therapeutica, it cannot be undone. The ROM of the biological grammar.

phase_2.split_element: A therapeutic protocol that branches into a plant-based arm (Phytoglyphica) and a fungal-based arm (Fungiglyphica)
split_outputs: Plant-based arm (e.g., salicin from Salix alba)
Fungal-based arm (e.g., penicillin from Penicillium chrysogenum)
phase_2.fuse_element: The combined therapeutic outcome that reconstitutes the original disease state as cured

phase_3.void_description: The uninscribed fourfold domain — no plant, fungus, animal, or therapy has been named. The state before any glyph is written.
phase_3.true_description: A successful therapeutic outcome — the disease is resolved, the patient is restored. The affirmative state of the curative cycle.
phase_3.false_description: A failed therapeutic outcome — the disease persists or worsens. The negative state of the curative cycle.
phase_3.both_description: A paradoxical therapeutic state — the disease is both present and absent, managed but not cured. The paradice of the chronic condition.

steps.domain_action: The Ars quadripartita is uninscribed — no plant, fungus, animal, or therapy exists yet. The void before any glyph.
Phytoglyphica emerges from the void — 147 plants are inscribed as distinct glyphs, each with its structural type.
Fungiglyphica emerges — 86 fungi are inscribed, each with its structural type, connected to plants via decomposition.
Animaglyphica emerges — 80 animals are inscribed, each with its structural type, connected to plants via ingestion.
Therapeutica is inscribed as the boundary — 10 therapies that close the system, containing all four domains.
A disease state enters the therapeutic fork. It splits into two arms: a plant-based arm (Phytoglyphica) and a fungal-based arm (Fungiglyphica).
The plant-based arm traverses Phytoglyphica — a specific plant species is selected and its active compound is extracted.
The plant-based arm succeeds — the compound shows efficacy against the disease. The true branch is affirmed.
The fungal-based arm traverses Fungiglyphica — a specific fungal species is selected and its metabolite is extracted.
The fungal-based arm fails — the metabolite shows no efficacy or causes toxicity. The false branch is negated.

The two arms reconstitute at the therapeutic endpoint. The successful plant-based arm and the failed fungal-based arm fuse into a single outcome: the disease is cured by the plant compound, with the fungal failure recorded as a negative result.
The successful therapy is canonized in the Therapeutica — a permanent record that this plant compound cures this disease. The protocol is fixed and cannot be undone.
The cured patient recognizes themselves as a distinct glyph in the Animaglyphica — the self-knowing identity of the healed organism.
The full chain is composed: plant → animal (ingestion) → therapy → cure. The sequential morphism from Phytoglyphica through Animaglyphica to Therapeutica is complete.
The cycle descends: the cured animal dies and is decomposed by fungi, returning matter to Fungiglyphica. The contravariant descent closes the biological loop.
A paradoxical state emerges — the disease is both present and absent in the system. The chronic patient lives with the disease managed but not cured, held in suspended resolution.
The system returns to its boundary. The Therapeutica contains all outcomes — success, failure, and paradox — within the closed frame of the Ars quadripartita.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Imscribing Grammar (IMASM) — translates biological structures into 12-primitive types and therapeutic operations into token sequences.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Frobenius functorial mappings between domains — e.g., Phytoglyphica → Animaglyphica via ingestion, Animaglyphica → Fungiglyphica via decomposition. Each mapping is a structural morphism that preserves the Frobenius condition.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The Therapeutica — a fixed, append-only store of 10 canonical therapies. Each therapy is a permanent record of a successful (or failed) curative protocol.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The trophic cycle — determines the order and timing of operations: plants grow, animals consume, fungi decompose, therapies intervene. The cycle is driven by ecological time.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Ars quadripartita itself — the canonical bootstrap store that contains all 147 plants, 86 fungi, 80 animals, and 10 therapies as a single, self-referential glyph system.

phase_6.cycle_cost: One trophic cycle — the energy consumed by a plant growing, an animal consuming, a fungus decomposing, and a therapy intervening.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The uninscribed fourfold domain — no plant, fungus, animal, or therapy has been named. The void before any glyph.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The fully inscribed Ars quadripartita — all 147 plants, 86 fungi, 80 animals, and 10 therapies are named and connected. The system is closed.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the trophic cycle is entropy-conservative because every decomposition (AREV) exactly reverses a consumption (AFWD), and every therapy (TANCH) closes the loop without net energy loss. The biological cycle is Frobenius-closed.
